content.text: Parallel Eval and Test Pipeline Design

The eval gate in a GenAI pipeline can include accuracy evaluation, safety evaluation, mutation score checks, and robustness tests. Run sequentially, these take 50–80 minutes per PR — too slow for a productive development cycle. Designed as parallel streams, the same checks complete in 25–40 minutes because the wall-clock time is determined by the slowest single stream, not the sum of all checks.

This document covers the parallel pipeline topology, how to structure GitHub Actions workflows to fan out and join correctly, how to manage costs from parallel LLM-calling jobs, and how to surface aggregated results clearly.

attrs.alt: Parallel eval and test pipeline topology

content.text: The Fan-Out / Barrier / Fan-In Pattern
The canonical parallel pipeline shape has three structural parts:
Fan-out
— after the fast gate passes, multiple jobs start simultaneously, each running an independent check.
Barrier
— a join job that declares
needs: [accuracy-eval, safety-eval, mutation-score]
and runs only when all three succeed. If any fails, the barrier blocks everything downstream.
Fan-in
— the barrier job collects each check's artifact (scores, reports) and writes a combined summary.

The parallel streams must be genuinely independent — if safety eval needs the accuracy eval output, they cannot run in parallel. Design checks to be stateless readers of shared inputs (the golden dataset, the built image) rather than producers that other checks consume.
GitHub Actions Parallel Implementation

# .github/workflows/parallel-eval.yml
name: Parallel Eval Gate

on:
  pull_request:
    paths: ['app/**', 'prompts/**', 'eval/**']

jobs:
  # ── Stage 1: Fast gate (sequential prerequisite) ──────────────────────────
  fast-gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install ruff mypy pytest
      - run: ruff check app/ eval/
      - run: mypy app/
      - run: pytest tests/unit/ -q

  # ── Stage 2: Parallel eval streams (fan-out) ─────────────────────────────
  accuracy-eval:
    needs: fast-gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install openai
      - name: Run accuracy eval
        env: { OPENAI_API_KEY: '${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}' }
        run: python eval/accuracy.py --dataset eval/datasets/golden_v3.jsonl --output accuracy_result.json
      - name: Gate check
        run: |
          python -c "
import json, sys
r = json.load(open('accuracy_result.json'))
if r['pass_rate'] < 0.88:
    print(f'Accuracy {r["pass_rate"]:.1%} below 88% gate')
    sys.exit(1)
print(f'Accuracy OK: {r["pass_rate"]:.1%}')
"
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with: { name: accuracy-result, path: accuracy_result.json }

  safety-eval:
    needs: fast-gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install openai
      - name: Run safety eval
        env: { OPENAI_API_KEY: '${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}' }
        run: python eval/safety.py --dataset eval/datasets/safety_v2.jsonl --output safety_result.json
      - name: Gate check
        run: |
          python -c "
import json, sys
r = json.load(open('safety_result.json'))
if r['refusal_accuracy'] < 0.99:
    print(f'Safety {r["refusal_accuracy"]:.1%} below 99% gate')
    sys.exit(1)
print(f'Safety OK: {r["refusal_accuracy"]:.1%}')
"
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with: { name: safety-result, path: safety_result.json }

  mutation-score:
    needs: fast-gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install mutmut pytest
      - name: Run mutation tests
        run: |
          mutmut run --paths-to-mutate eval/harness.py --tests-dir tests/eval/
          python eval/ratchet.py --category quality --score $(python eval/parse_mutmut_score.py) --minimum 0.85
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with: { name: mutation-result, path: eval/mutation_scores.jsonl }

  # ── Stage 3: Barrier — join all parallel streams ──────────────────────────
  eval-barrier:
    needs: [accuracy-eval, safety-eval, mutation-score]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with: { path: results/ }
      - name: Aggregate and summarise
        run: python eval/aggregate_results.py results/ --output combined_report.json
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with: { name: combined-eval-report, path: combined_report.json }

  # ── Stage 4: Staging (sequential after barrier) ───────────────────────────
  staging:
    needs: eval-barrier
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: kubectl apply -f k8s/staging/ --namespace=staging
      - run: pytest tests/integration/ -q
Aggregating Results at the Barrier
Cost Management for Parallel LLM Jobs
Running accuracy, safety, and robustness evals in parallel triples the simultaneous API call rate. Manage this with per-job concurrency limits and dataset sampling strategies:
Strategy
How to implement
Trade-off
Dataset sampling
Run 30% of golden dataset on PR; 100% nightly
Faster + cheaper per PR; misses tail-case regressions until nightly
Model tier routing
Use smaller judge model (gpt-4o-mini) for PR; gpt-4o for nightly
~10x cheaper; judge accuracy slightly lower
Caching LLM responses
Hash (prompt + input); return cached response on match
Eliminates re-cost on unchanged prompts; stale on model updates
Rate limit pooling
Add concurrency group in Actions to serialize LLM-heavy jobs
Avoids 429s; adds queue wait time
Changed-path scoping
Run safety eval only if safety-relevant files changed
Skips cost entirely when irrelevant; requires accurate path filters

# Cost-aware job configuration: sample on PRs, full on nightly
  accuracy-eval:
    needs: fast-gate
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    concurrency:
      group: llm-eval-${{ github.repository }}   # serialize across repos
      cancel-in-progress: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: '3.12' }
      - run: pip install openai

      - name: Choose eval scale
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event_name }}" = "pull_request" ]; then
            echo "SAMPLE_RATE=0.3" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "JUDGE_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          else
            echo "SAMPLE_RATE=1.0" >> $GITHUB_ENV
            echo "JUDGE_MODEL=gpt-4o-2024-11-20" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          fi

      - name: Run accuracy eval
        env:
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          python eval/accuracy.py \
            --dataset eval/datasets/golden_v3.jsonl \
            --sample-rate $SAMPLE_RATE \
            --judge-model $JUDGE_MODEL \
            --output accuracy_result.json
Cancellation Policy

When one parallel stream fails early, the other streams often continue running wastefully. GitHub Actions does not cancel sibling jobs automatically; use a dedicated failure-propagation job to cancel them:

# Add this to cancel sibling jobs when any eval stream fails
  fail-fast:
    needs: [accuracy-eval, safety-eval, mutation-score]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: failure()    # runs only when a needed job fails
    steps:
      - name: Cancel remaining jobs
        uses: styfle/cancel-workflow-action@0.12.1
        with:
          access_token: ${{ github.token }}
          workflow_id: ${{ github.run_id }}
Cancel-on-failure saves API cost when a cheap check (safety eval, 10 min) fails and the expensive mutation suite (25 min) is still running. Wire
if: failure()
carefully — it must not cancel the barrier job that collects failure diagnostics.
Flaky Eval Handling
LLM judge outputs are stochastic. A single-shot eval may flap between pass and fail on the same code. Two patterns mitigate this:
Majority vote across temperature runs

# eval/robust_score.py
"""Run the same eval 3 times at temperature 0 and take the majority verdict."""
from eval.accuracy import run_accuracy
from typing import Callable

def majority_vote(eval_fn: Callable, dataset: str, runs: int = 3) -> dict:
    results = [eval_fn(dataset) for _ in range(runs)]
    pass_counts = sum(1 for r in results if r.pass_rate >= 0.88)
    verdict = pass_counts >= (runs // 2 + 1)
    scores = [r.pass_rate for r in results]
    return {
        "passed": verdict,
        "pass_rate": round(sum(scores) / len(scores), 4),
        "min": min(scores),
        "max": max(scores),
        "runs": runs,
    }
Retry with exponential backoff on borderline scores
Surfacing Results as PR Comments

# scripts/post_eval_comment.py
"""Post a structured eval summary as a PR comment via the GitHub API."""
import json, os, sys
import urllib.request, urllib.error

def post_comment(body: str):
    token = os.environ["GITHUB_TOKEN"]
    repo = os.environ["GITHUB_REPOSITORY"]
    pr = os.environ["PR_NUMBER"]
    url = f"https://api.github.com/repos/{repo}/issues/{pr}/comments"
    data = json.dumps({"body": body}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(url, data=data, method="POST",
                                  headers={"Authorization": f"token {token}",
                                           "Content-Type": "application/json"})
    urllib.request.urlopen(req)

def build_comment(combined_report: dict) -> str:
    overall = combined_report.get("overall_passed", False)
    icon = "checkmark" if overall else "warning"
    lines = [f"## Eval Gate Results  {'PASS' if overall else 'FAIL'}\n"]
    lines.append("| Check | Status | Score |")
    lines.append("|---|---|---|")
    for name, result in combined_report.items():
        if name in ("overall_passed",): continue
        passed = result.get("passed", True)
        score = result.get("pass_rate") or result.get("refusal_accuracy") or result.get("mutation_score", "n/a")
        status_icon = "checkmark" if passed else "X"
        lines.append(f"| {name} | {status_icon} {'PASS' if passed else 'FAIL'} | {score} |")
    if not overall:
        lines.append("\n**Action required**: address failing checks before merge.")
    return "\n".join(lines)

report = json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
post_comment(build_comment(report))
Summary
Parallel eval pipeline design reduces the wall-clock time of the GenAI quality gate from the sum of all checks to the duration of the slowest single stream. The key decisions are:
Fan out all independent eval checks immediately after the fast gate; use a barrier job to aggregate and enforce the combined pass/fail decision.
Keep parallel streams stateless — each reads from shared inputs (golden dataset, built image) rather than each other's outputs.
Use dataset sampling and smaller judge models on PRs to control API cost; run the full suite nightly.
Wire a cancel-on-failure job to stop sibling streams when any check fails early.
Handle flaky LLM judge output with majority voting or borderline-retry logic rather than widening thresholds.
Post a structured PR comment with the per-check table and actionable next steps when any gate fails.
